responseMessageContents.text:
Done. Here's the summary:

**Files changed:**
1. `expo/i18n/translations.ts`
2. `expo/app/(tabs)/add/index.tsx`
3. `expo/app/edit-equipment.tsx`

**Validation messages implemented (AR + EN):**

| Field | Arabic | English |
|-------|--------|---------|
| titleAr empty | العنوان العربي مطلوب | Arabic title is required |
| titleEn empty | العنوان الإنجليزي مطلوب | English title is required |
| category missing | اختر الفئة | Please select a category |
| city missing | اختر المدينة | Please select a city |
| price invalid | أدخل سعر صحيح | Please enter a valid price |
| images empty | أضف صورة واحدة على الأقل | Please add at least one image |
| name missing | الاسم مطلوب | Name is required |
| phone missing | رقم الجوال مطلوب | Phone number is required |
| unexpected error | حدث خطأ غير متوقع، حاول مرة أخرى | An unexpected error occurred, please try again |

**What changed:**
- **Create listing** (`add/index.tsx`): Each required field is now validated individually before any upload/Firestore write. Generic `t('try_again')` replaced with specific per-field messages. Price is validated as a positive number. Catch block now uses `console.error` and shows the actual error message when available, falling back to `unexpected_error`.
- **Edit equipment** (`edit-equipment.tsx`): Same per-field validation and improved error handling applied.
- **Profile**: No edit form exists yet — the edit profile button is currently non-functional. Validation keys (`validation_name_required`, `validation_phone_required`) are already added to i18n and ready to use when the profile edit form is built.

**Confirmation:** Generic error alerts are now fully replaced with specific validation messages in both equipment creation and editing flows.

responseMessageContents.title: Updated the app to show clear instructions instead of generic error messages when adding or editing equipment listings.
responseMessageContents.description: Replaced the general "An error occurred" message with specific guidance in both Arabic and English. Now, if a user forgets a title, category, city, or price, the app will tell them exactly what is missing before they try to save their changes. This ensures a smoother experience when publishing or updating equipment.
